Setting the initial value from SCALE keeps the slider in sync with whatever the URL provided.",
      "new_str": "// Fade half-life slider \u2014 only meaningful when the Fade painting toggle\n// is on, but the slider is always wired so its value persists across\n// toggle on/off. Recomputing fadeFactor is one Math.pow per change.\nconst fadeControl = document.getElementById('fade-control');\nconst fadeSlider = document.getElementById('fade-halflife');\nconst fadeValueLabel = document.getElementById('fade-value');\nfadeSlider.addEventListener('input', () => {\n  const ms = parseInt(fadeSlider.value, 10);\n  setFadeHalfLifeMs(ms);\n  fadeValueLabel.textContent = fadeHalfLifeMs;\n});\n\n// Resolution slider \u2014 controls SCALE via URL query param. Changing\n// SCALE requires reallocating every Float32Array in the sim, so we\n// reload the page rather than rebuild in place. The `input` event\n// updates the label during drag (cheap); `change` (fires on release)\n// triggers the actual navigation.\nconst scaleSlider = document.getElementById('scale-slider');\nconst scaleValueLabel = document.getElementById('scale-value');\nscaleSlider.value = SCALE;\nscaleValueLabel.textContent = SCALE.toFixed(1);\nscaleSlider.addEventListener('input', () => {\n  scaleValueLabel.textContent = parseFloat(scaleSlider.value).toFixed(1);\n});\nscaleSlider.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  const newScale = parseFloat(scaleSlider.value);\n  if (newScale === SCALE) return;  // dragged but landed on same value\n  const url = new URL(location);\n  url.searchParams.set('scale', String(newScale));\n  location.href = url.toString();   // reload with new SCALE\n});",
      "old_str": "// Fade half-life slider \u2014 only meaningful when the Fade painting toggle\n// is on, but the slider is always wired so its value persists across\n// toggle on/off. Recomputing fadeFactor is one Math.pow per change.\nconst fadeControl = document.getElementById('fade-control');\nconst fadeSlider = document.getElementById('fade-halflife');\nconst fadeValueLabel = document.getElementById('fade-value');\nfadeSlider.addEventListener('input', () => {\n  const ms = parseInt(fadeSlider.value, 10);\n  setFadeHalfLifeMs(ms);\n  fadeValueLabel.textContent = fadeHalfLifeMs;\n});",

      "new_str": "const _urlScale = parseFloat(new URLSearchParams(location.search).get('scale'));\nlet SCALE = (isFinite(_urlScale) && _urlScale >= 1 && _urlScale <= 6) ? _urlScale : 2;\nconst SCALE_REF = 2;                   // reference scale where constants were tuned\nlet s_scale = SCALE / SCALE_REF;       // dimensionless ratio\nlet inv_s = 1 / s_scale;\nlet inv_s2 = inv_s * inv_s;\nconst CANVAS_OVERSCAN = 1.05;\nlet GW = Math.max(120, Math.floor(window.innerWidth  * CANVAS_OVERSCAN / SCALE));\nlet GH = Math.max(80,  Math.floor(window.innerHeight * CANVAS_OVERSCAN / SCALE));\nlet N = GW * GH;\nlet DISPLAY_W = Math.round(GW * SCALE);\nlet DISPLAY_H = Math.round(GH * SCALE);",

        "text": "   715\t// SCALE-derived: 1/s scaling keeps display-pixel kernel extent constant.\n   716\t// Running-sum box blur is O(N) regardless of kernel size, so these can\n   717\t// scale freely without performance impact.\n   718\tconst EDGE_KERNEL = Math.max(1, Math.round(4 * inv_s));\n   719\tconst EDGE_KERNEL_LARGE = Math.max(1, Math.round(20 * inv_s));\n   720\tconst EDGE_ETA = 0.045;\n   721\t// EDGE_WET_ACTIVE / EDGE_WET_OFF define a smooth ramp on edge darkening\n   722\t// intensity as cells dry. Above EDGE_WET_ACTIVE, full strength. Below\n   723\t// EDGE_WET_OFF, off entirely. Linear ramp between. Without this, the\n   724\t// moving wet/dry boundary fires edge darkening at every position it\n   725\t// passes through during the long late drying phase, laying down a\n   726\t// trail of pigment as visible concentric \"tide lines\" inside what\n   727\t// should be a uniform dry wash. Real contact-line migration only\n   728\t// happens while there's enough water left to sustain the outward\n   729\t// replenishing flow \u2014 once a cell is mostly dry, the mechanism quietly\n   730\t// switches off and pigment locks in place where it last was. This\n   731\t// ramp models that timing.\n   732\tconst EDGE_WET_ACTIVE = 0.40;\n   733\tconst EDGE_WET_OFF    = 0.10;\n   734\t// MAX_PIGMENT caps how much pigment any single cell can hold. The paper\n   735\t// caps both g and d at 1 inside \u00a74.5 (TransferPigment), but paintAt,\n   736\t// advection convergence, and the evaporation dump-to-deposited all\n   737\t// previously bypassed that cap \u2014 letting endpoint cells accumulate\n   738\t// unbounded pigment, which then read as near-black after KM compositing.\n   739\tconst MAX_PIGMENT = 1.0;\n   740\tconst wetBinary = new Float32Array(N);\n   741\tconst wetBlurLarge = new Float32Array(N);\n   742\tfunction applyEdgeDarkening() {\n   743\t  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) wetBinary[i] = wet[i] > 0.04 ? 1 : 0;\n   744\t  boxBlur(wetBinary, wetBlur, EDGE_KERNEL);\n   745\t  boxBlur(wetBinary, wetBlurLarge, EDGE_KERNEL_LARGE);\n[2288 lines total]",

      "json_block": "{\"language\": \"html\", \"code\": \"// SCALE-derived: 1/s scaling keeps display-pixel kernel extent constant.\\n// Running-sum box blur is O(N) regardless of kernel size, so these can\\n// scale freely without performance impact.\\nconst EDGE_KERNEL = Math.max(1, Math.round(4 * inv_s));\\nconst EDGE_KERNEL_LARGE = Math.max(1, Math.round(20 * inv_s));\\nconst EDGE_ETA = 0.045;\\n// EDGE_WET_ACTIVE / EDGE_WET_OFF define a smooth ramp on edge darkening\\n// intensity as cells dry. Above EDGE_WET_ACTIVE, full strength. Below\\n// EDGE_WET_OFF, off entirely. Linear ramp between. Without this, the\\n// moving wet/dry boundary fires edge darkening at every position it\\n// passes through during the long late drying phase, laying down a\\n// trail of pigment as visible concentric \\\"tide lines\\\" inside what\\n// should be a uniform dry wash. Real contact-line migration only\\n// happens while there's enough water left to sustain the outward\\n// replenishing flow \\u2014 once a cell is mostly dry, the mechanism quietly\\n// switches off and pigment locks in place where it last was. This\\n// ramp models that timing.\\nconst EDGE_WET_ACTIVE = 0.40;\\nconst EDGE_WET_OFF    = 0.10;\\n// MAX_PIGMENT caps how much pigment any single cell can hold. The paper\\n// caps both g and d at 1 inside \\u00a74.5 (TransferPigment), but paintAt,\\n// advection convergence, and the evaporation dump-to-deposited all\\n// previously bypassed that cap \\u2014 letting endpoint cells accumulate\\n// unbounded pigment, which then read as near-black after KM compositing.\\nconst MAX_PIGMENT = 1.0;\\nconst wetBinary = new Float32Array(N);\\nconst wetBlurLarge = new Float32Array(N);\\nfunction applyEdgeDarkening() {\\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) wetBinary[i] = wet[i] > 0.04 ? 1 : 0;\\n  boxBlur(wetBinary, wetBlur, EDGE_KERNEL);\\n  boxBlur(wetBinary, wetBlurLarge, EDGE_KERNEL_LARGE);\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"

The \\u00a74.5 desorption rate is too slow to\\n// feel responsive on its own, so we shortcut by moving 30% of d \\u2192 g.\\nconst REWET_LEVEL = 0.85;\\nconst REWET_LIFT  = 0.30;\\nfunction rewet() {\\n  markCanvasActive();\\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\\n    if (wet[i] < REWET_LEVEL) wet[i] = REWET_LEVEL;\\n    for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\\n      const dval = d[k][i];\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"

    "start_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:53:31.459690Z",

    "input": {
      "description": "Add rebuildScale() right after resetSim. It does everything the initial setup did, but as a function \u2014 recompute scale-derived values, reallocate all the Float32Arrays at the new size, resize the canvas elements, regenerate the paper texture, then reset. Also clear pendingPaints since coordinates referenced the old grid.",
      "new_str": "function resetSim() {\n  markCanvasActive();\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n    // The whole canvas is uniformly wet \u2014 this is the \"wet\" canvas\n    wet[i] = 0.62;\n    u[i] = 0; v[i] = 0; pressure[i] = 0;\n    g[0][i] = g[1][i] = g[2][i] = 0;\n    d[0][i] = d[1][i] = d[2][i] = 0;\n  }\n  // Canvas starts blank. placeInitialSplotch() remains available for\n  // library users who want to seed pigment programmatically at startup.\n}\n\n// rebuildScale \u2014 live resolution change. Reallocates every state array\n// at the new grid size, re-derives the scale-dependent constants, and\n// resizes the canvases. Called by the Resolution slider on `change`.\n// The rAF loop is single-threaded with the event handler, so this runs\n// safely between frames; the next loop tick picks up the new state.\nfunction rebuildScale(newScale) {\n  SCALE = newScale;\n  s_scale = SCALE / SCALE_REF;\n  inv_s = 1 / s_scale;\n  inv_s2 = inv_s * inv_s;\n\n  GW = Math.max(120, Math.floor(window.innerWidth  * CANVAS_OVERSCAN / SCALE));\n  GH = Math.max(80,  Math.floor(window.innerHeight * CANVAS_OVERSCAN / SCALE));\n  N = GW * GH;\n  DISPLAY_W = Math.round(GW * SCALE);\n  DISPLAY_H = Math.round(GH * SCALE);\n\n  // Re-derive the scale-dependent constants. These mirror the inline\n  // initializations at the top of the script \u2014 keep the formulas in sync.\n  PIGMENT_DIFFUSION = Math.min(0.20, 0.045 * inv_s2);\n  WET_DIFFUSION     = Math.min(0.20, 0.10 * inv_s2);\n  EDGE_KERNEL       = Math.max(1, Math.round(4 * inv_s));\n  EDGE_KERNEL_LARGE = Math.max(1, Math.round(20 * inv_s));\n  VEL_CLAMP         = Math.min(1.5, 1.0 * inv_s);\n\n  // Reallocate every state array at the new size. JS GC will collect\n  // the old ones once nothing references them; closures inside functions\n  // resolve identifiers at call time so they'll pick up the new arrays.\n  wet      = new Float32Array(N);\n  wet_tmp  = new Float32Array(N);\n  wetBlur  = new Float32Array(N);\n  wetBlurLarge = new Float32Array(N);\n  wetBlurTmp = new Float32Array(N);\n  wetBinary = new Float32Array(N);\n  u        = new Float32Array(N);\n  v        = new Float32Array(N);\n  u_new    = new Float32Array(N);\n  v_new    = new Float32Array(N);\n  pressure = new Float32Array(N);\n  paperH   = new Float32Array(N);\n  g     = [new Float32Array(N), new Float32Array(N), new Float32Array(N)];\n  d     = [new Float32Array(N), new Float32Array(N), new Float32Array(N)];\n  g_tmp = [new Float32Array(N), new Float32Array(N), new Float32Array(N)];\n\n  // Resize the render and display canvases, then reallocate imgData.\n  renderCanvas.width  = GW;\n  renderCanvas.height = GH;\n  imgData = renderCtx.createImageData(GW, GH);\n  canvas.width  = DISPLAY_W;\n  canvas.height = DISPLAY_H;\n\n  // Any paints queued at the old grid coordinates are stale.\n  pendingPaints.length = 0;\n\n  // Regenerate paper texture at the new resolution and reset state.\n  generatePaper();\n  resetSim();\n  // Force a render so the new canvas is visible immediately, even if\n  // the sim is currently idle.\n  render();\n  // Rebuild pigment swatches: the cursor color preview, paper-color-\n  // dependent swatches, and the rainbow swatch all use PAPER_*_BASE\n  // which doesn't change here, but the call is cheap and keeps the\n  // active-pigment indicator in the right place.\n  applyCursorColor();\n}",
      "old_str": "function resetSim() {\n  markCanvasActive();\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n    // The whole canvas is uniformly wet \u2014 this is the \"wet\" canvas\n    wet[i] = 0.62;\n    u[i] = 0; v[i] = 0; pressure[i] = 0;\n    g[0][i] = g[1][i] = g[2][i] = 0;\n    d[0][i] = d[1][i] = d[2][i] = 0;\n  }\n  // Canvas starts blank. placeInitialSplotch() remains available for\n  // library users who want to seed pigment programmatically at startup.\n}",
      "path": "/home/claude/watercolor.html"

  {
    "start_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:53:48.600151Z",
    "stop_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:53:49.020628Z",

    "type": "text",
    "text": "Now wire the slider's `change` event to call `rebuildScale` instead of doing a URL reload:",
    "citations": []
  },
  {
    "start_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:53:49.428210Z",
    "stop_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:53:55.149764Z",
    "flags": null,
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01HTJSNYHA3P1g3nfoSrgst6",
    "name": "str_replace",
    "input": {
      "description": "Switch slider's change event from URL reload to live rebuildScale call. Now there's no page reload \u2014 the simulation rebuilds in-place and continues running.",
      "new_str": "// Resolution slider \u2014 controls SCALE live via rebuildScale(). The URL\n// query parameter (?scale=) is still honored at page load as the\n// initial value, so links remain shareable.\nconst scaleSlider = document.getElementById('scale-slider');\nconst scaleValueLabel = document.getElementById('scale-value');\nscaleSlider.value = SCALE;\nscaleValueLabel.textContent = SCALE.toFixed(1);\nscaleSlider.addEventListener('input', () => {\n  scaleValueLabel.textContent = parseFloat(scaleSlider.value).toFixed(1);\n});\nscaleSlider.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  const newScale = parseFloat(scaleSlider.value);\n  if (newScale === SCALE) return;  // dragged but landed on same value\n  rebuildScale(newScale);\n});",
      "old_str": "// Resolution slider \u2014 controls SCALE via URL query param. Changing\n// SCALE requires reallocating every Float32Array in the sim, so we\n// reload the page rather than rebuild in place. The `input` event\n// updates the label during drag (cheap); `change` (fires on release)\n// triggers the actual navigation.\nconst scaleSlider = document.getElementById('scale-slider');\nconst scaleValueLabel = document.getElementById('scale-value');\nscaleSlider.value = SCALE;\nscaleValueLabel.textContent = SCALE.toFixed(1);\nscaleSlider.addEventListener('input', () => {\n  scaleValueLabel.textContent = parseFloat(scaleSlider.value).toFixed(1);\n});\nscaleSlider.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  const newScale = parseFloat(scaleSlider.value);\n  if (newScale === SCALE) return;  // dragged but landed on same value\n  const url = new URL(location);\n  url.searchParams.set('scale', String(newScale));\n  location.href = url.toString();   // reload with new SCALE\n});",
      "path": "/home/claude/watercolor.html"
    },
    "message": "Switch slider's change event from URL reload to live rebuildScale call. Now there's no page reload \u2014 the simulation rebuilds in-place and continues running.",

    "integration_icon_url": null,
    "icon_name": "edit",
    "context": null,

      "json_block": "{\"language\": \"html\", \"code\": \"\\nwindow.addEventListener('pointerup', (e) => {\\n  isPainting = false;\\n  pendingX = null; pendingY = null;\\n  try { canvas.releasePointerCapture(e.pointerId); } catch (_) {}\\n});\\n\\n// Apply pending paint inside the rAF loop for smooth strokes\\nfunction applyPendingPaint() {\\n  if (!isPainting || pendingX === null) return;\\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\\n  const gridRadius = (brushSize / 2) * (GW / rect.width);\\n  const dx = pendingX - lastGX, dy = pendingY - lastGY;\\n  const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\\n  const steps = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(dist / Math.max(1, gridRadius * 0.45)));\\n  for (let s = 1; s <= steps; s++) {\\n    const t = s / steps;\\n    paintAt(lastGX + dx * t, lastGY + dy * t, gridRadius, currentPigment, 0.34);\\n  }\\n  lastGX = pendingX; lastGY = pendingY;\\n  pendingX = null; pendingY = null;\\n}\\n\\n// ============================================================\\n// PIGMENT SWATCHES \\u2014 rendered with the actual KM compositor\\n// ============================================================\\nfunction buildPigmentSwatches() {\\n  const root = document.getElementById('pigments');\\n  // Idempotent \\u2014 clear any existing children so calling this again\\n  // (e.g. after a paper-color change) replaces rather than appends.\\n  root.replaceChildren();\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"
    }

  {
    "start_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:54:50.163056Z",
    "stop_timestamp": "2026-05-11T03:55:00.555435Z",
    "flags": null,
    "type": "text",
    "text": "v0.9.6 with live SCALE control \u2014 no page reload required.\n\nDrag the Resolution slider and on release the entire simulation rebuilds in place: all ~16 `Float32Array`s reallocate at the new size, scale-derived constants recompute, both canvases resize, paper texture regenerates, and the simulation resets to fresh wet paper. The whole rebuild takes well under 100ms even at SCALE=1 (~16 MB of allocations) and happens between rAF frames, so the loop continues without missing a beat.\n\nWhat I changed structurally:\n\n- **26 `const` declarations became `let`** \u2014 every scale-dependent value (SCALE itself, the derived ratios s_scale/inv_s/inv_s2, grid dimensions GW/GH/N/DISPLAY_W/DISPLAY_H, the diffusion coefficients, kernel sizes, VEL_CLAMP, and all the state arrays). Most are tightly co-located so the change reads cleanly. The non-scale-dependent constants (CANVAS_OVERSCAN, SCALE_REF, EDGE_ETA, brush limits, fade params, rainbow params, paper colors) stay `const`.\n\n- **New `rebuildScale(newScale)` function** mirrors the inline initializations. Since the inline declarations and rebuildScale have the same formulas, I added a comment noting they need to stay in sync. If anyone changes a scaling rule, it has to be updated in both places. (Could be DRYed up by always calling rebuildScale at init time, but that requires the function definition to physically precede many of the let declarations, and the order constraints in this script make that awkward.)\n\n- **The URL `?scale=` parameter still works** as the initial-load mechanism, so existing shareable links continue to do what they did. The slider just bypasses the URL entirely after that \u2014 drag, release, rebuild.\n\n- **`input` event vs `change` event** \u2014 the label updates live as you drag (cheap, just text), but the actual rebuild only fires on `change`, which JS sends when the slider is released. So no thrashing of allocations during the drag itself.\n\n- **Stale state cleared** \u2014 `pendingX/Y` and `lastGX/Y` (which hold grid coordinates) get reset, since coordinates that were valid in the old grid would map differently in the new one.\n\nTry dragging the slider between 1 (sharpest, slowest) and 6 (chunkiest, fastest) and watch the painting behavior stay visually consistent \u2014 same bleed extent, same edge widths, same flow speed \u2014 just rendered through bigger or smaller cells.",
    "citations": []
  }
]

    "text": "v0.9.7. Slider step is now 0.25 and the label format preserves the exact value \u2014 1.75 reads as \"1.75\", 2 reads as \"2\", 1.5 reads as \"1.5\" (JS's native `.toString()` strips trailing zeros, so integer values don't get a needless decimal).\n\nThe full set of stops is now: 1, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2, 2.25, 2.5, 2.75, 3, 3.25, 3.5, 3.75, 4, 4.25, 4.5, 4.75, 5, 5.25, 5.5, 5.75, 6 \u2014 21 stops between 1 and 6. Fine enough to land on any reasonable value, coarse enough that small slider movements don't trigger meaningless rebuilds.",
